When departing during travels, Rousseau thought only of a charming walk.
Rousseau was vexed when offered a place in a carriage or accosted on the road because it interrupted his imaginative thoughts.
Rousseau wore a short cloak about his shoulder which Madam de Warens had obliged him to put on.
Rousseau would willingly have been excused from his companions' diversions.
When Rousseau has a sum of money he keeps it by him for want of knowing how to dispose of it to his satisfaction.
Rousseau's companions wished him to partake of their diversions.

39.question_text: How does Rousseau's suspicion of institutional power influence his strategic decisions about where to seek refuge?
fact_texts: Distant expectation rarely has strength enough to influence Rousseau's actions.
Rousseau felt the influence of his passions without knowing whither they would conduct him.
Rousseau's vanity rendered his resolution to defend his opinion unnecessary.
Rousseau's apparent certainty of death animated his inclination for improvement.
When Rousseau has a sum of money he keeps it by him for want of knowing how to dispose of it to his satisfaction.
Rousseau's spending is in subservience to his pleasures.
Rousseau's friends were irresolute about whether to make him a watchmaker, lawyer, or minister.
Despite Rousseau's extreme aversion to restraint, his recollection of his studies was never attended with disgust.
The dread of seeing the end of his independence makes Rousseau unwilling to part with his money.
While under the influence of his passions, nothing can equal Rousseau's impetuosity.
